anchored_threads.explanation: 2.50 is the central definitional verse of the karma-yoga thread. The verse defines yoga as kauśalam in action — directly addressing the chapter thesis claim that karma-yoga is 'shipping discipline... how to run code without grasping at outcomes'. The engineering analog operationalizes the definition (artifact-density across similar-task classes) and stays anchored on the karma-yoga thread without drifting into sthita-prajña territory.

ordering: 2.50 follows 2.49 (which ranks buddhi-yoga above fruit-driven action) and precedes 2.51 (which names the destination padam anāmayam). The block 2.49-2.51 builds: ranking (2.49) → mechanism + definition (2.50) → destination (2.51). The 2.50 analog sits at the central position contributing the definitional move that anchors both the upstream ranking and the downstream destination.
verdict_reason: Strongly supports the chapter thesis. yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam is the chapter's most-quoted line and the engineering analog handles it with the rigor the chapter brief requires. The 'first-principles' tag connects to the sāṅkhya thread (essence-vs-implementation); 'shipping-discipline' connects to the karma-yoga thread; 'non-attachment-to-praise' foreshadows the sthita-prajña thread. Pass.
